items.title: Show HN: our coding agent now runs tests before proposing a diff
items.body: We rebuilt our coding agent so that it always runs the project test suite before proposing a diff. In our internal benchmark of 200 tasks, the test-first loop reduced regressions by 38% compared to the previous propose-then-test flow. The agent now refuses to open a PR when tests fail.

items.title: Test-Driven Repair Loops for LLM Coding Agents
items.body: We study repair loops where an LLM coding agent iterates against a test oracle. Across three open-source repositories, agents that gate diffs on passing tests achieve higher merge rates than agents that do not. We caution that flaky tests can cause the loop to discard otherwise-correct patches.

items.title: Indirect prompt injection via retrieved web content remains unsolved
items.body: Researchers demonstrated that agents which fetch untrusted web pages remain vulnerable to indirect prompt injection. Wrapping retrieved content in delimiters helps but does not eliminate the risk; the most reliable mitigation observed was to strip imperative instructions from retrieved text before passing it to the model.

items.title: OWASP LLM Top 10: prompt injection stays at number one
items.body: In the latest revision, prompt injection remains the top-ranked risk for LLM applications. The guidance recommends treating all external content as untrusted and isolating it from instruction context.
